interactions.summary: The host asks what secrets Antarctica and other hidden-history sites may be covering up.

Jiang answers that mainstream human history is false, that prior advanced civilizations existed and were destroyed by cataclysm, and that elites suppress this because it would upend materialist evolution narratives.
The host closes by asking for another conversation, and Jiang agrees to speak again around the weekend of March 9 after returning to Beijing.

speaker_notes.note: Jiang's child briefly enters the scene mid-answer, but the interruption does not alter the substantive claims about lost civilizations.

claims.claim: Jiang says the standard story of human development is false because recorded history covers only a small fraction of Homo sapiens existence.
Jiang says sites such as Gobekli Tepe and the Amazon imply prior civilizations whose existence would destroy the conventional narrative if fully revealed.
Jiang says earlier civilizations were probably destroyed by flood or geomagnetic-excursion cycles and that the pyramids prove advanced capacities unlike our own.
Jiang says elites know about these prior civilizations and conceal them because such knowledge would collapse evolution and materialist accounts of the universe.
Jiang agrees to continue the conversation around the weekend of March 9, when he expects to be back in Beijing and freer to talk.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang opens the hidden-history frame by declaring the taught history of humanity fake.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the history that we've been taught ... it's all fake
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the packet's highest-heat line and would likely survive into the public read.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang uses the term for catastrophic pole shifts that he thinks periodically destroy civilizations.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang schedules a follow-up conversation for around the weekend of March 9 and says he will be back in Beijing by then.

uncertainty_notes.note: This packet is highly speculative and framed as Jiang's hidden-history model rather than as evidence-grounded archaeology inside the source.
